full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understand the three-stage update pipeline in Algorithm 1
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Can you help me trace through Algorithm 1 of the TC-GMM paper step by step, particularly how the fixed-complexity update in Eq 7-8 connects to the model splitting in Eq 9-10?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Helped me see that the E_i terms in Eq 7 are the link between stages — they're reused in Eq 9 for the difference component.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Cross-referenced Eq 3 and Eq 7 from the paper to confirm E_i = sum_j p(i|x_j).

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Understand why Historical GMM enables dynamic complexity
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: Why can't a single new data point ever justify increasing the number of Gaussian components in an incremental GMM? What does the Historical GMM solve?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Clarified the information-theoretic argument: one point adds O(1) evidence but complexity increases by O(D^2) parameters.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Verified against Section 1 problem challenges paragraph in the paper.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Why can't a single new data point ever justify increasing the number of Gaussian components in an incremental GMM? What does the Historical GMM solve?
top_5_prompts.why_important: Directly unlocked my understanding of the paper's core motivation — without this I could not have written the step 3 explanation.

top_5_prompts.prompt: Can you help me trace through Algorithm 1 of the TC-GMM paper step by step...
top_5_prompts.why_important: Helped me organise the step-by-step description and correctly identify that E_i is the memory-efficient sufficient statistic.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Part B.
